title: shebanq-mcp: a Model Context Protocol server for querying the BHSA Hebrew Bible in plain language
description:
shebanq-mcp is a Model Context Protocol (MCP) server that lets biblical-studies scholars query the BHSA Hebrew Bible, the ETCBC linguistic database behind SHEBANQ, in plain language. A question is translated into an Emdros MQL query, validated against the BHSA feature catalogue, and run on a local Emdros engine; the server returns both the query and the real results. The design keeps the query visible and checkable rather than hidden. Every result carries the exact MQL that produced it, queries are validated before they run, and empty results are reported honestly. The goal is to lower the barrier to MQL for teaching and research without removing the scholarly act of reading and judging a query: AI as a way in, not a way around. The server exposes three tools (search_bhsa, run_mql, lookup_feature), runs read-only, and can be used from any MCP client or through a hosted web demo. It builds on the BHSA dataset (ETCBC), the Emdros text database engine, and SHEBANQ.

Live demo: https://shebanq-web.onrender.com

What's new in v0.2.2:

  • The web demo's “include the reference” checkbox no longer re-translates the question. A question is translated once; the checkbox toggles between the flat query and the verse-wrapped query in place, so the same question can no longer yield a different query (or different results) just because the box was toggled.

What's new in v0.2.1:

  • Fixed a crash on multi-block queries: a query with two sibling sub-blocks that each request features (for example a predicate verb and its complement, as in “spoke with”) aborted the query worker. Each matched object is now read with exactly its own feature list.
  • The web demo's “include the reference” option now strips a verse wrapper when unchecked, instead of only adding one when checked, so an unchecked box reliably yields a flat query.
